vix.note: The VIX rose to 19.72, up about 4.4 percent on the day, a modest risk-off uptick that still sits far below the year high near 35 and points to rotation out of megacaps and into memory names rather than broad fear.

macro: Apple's price hikes turned the AI-memory boom into a margin scare, splitting tech as memory makers soared while device makers and hyperscalers sold off. Apple fell 6.1 percent after raising Mac and iPad prices by up to 20 percent to offset surging memory and storage costs, while Micron's record quarter and sold-out 2026 high-bandwidth-memory supply lifted the semis ETF 2.9 percent. Megacaps tied to heavy AI capex lagged, with Microsoft down 3.5 percent in a historic June rout and Amazon off 3.1 percent, yet the broad tape stayed orderly as the Dow rose 0.14 percent and the VIX held near 19.7. Gold and silver firmed and oil rebounded almost 3 percent, while Bitcoin slid back below 60k.
action: Lean into the memory super-cycle and the chip dip (SMH, INTC) over the hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN) that are paying for AI capex, keep quality ballast in BRKB, SPY and DIA, and wait for Apple to base before bottom-fishing the megacap margin scare.

long.h: Apple Hikes Mac and iPad Prices Up to 20% as AI Memory Shortage Lifts Component Costs
long.p: Apple raised hardware prices for its first broad increase in years to offset soaring memory and storage costs driven by AI data-center demand. The move reframes the AI memory boom as a margin problem for device makers, not just a tailwind for chip suppliers.

long.h: Micron's Record Quarter Confirms the AI Memory Super-Cycle, With 2026 HBM Supply Sold Out
long.p: Micron posted a blowout quarter on surging high-bandwidth-memory demand, and management said its entire calendar 2026 HBM supply is already reserved. The result validates a multi-year memory up-cycle that lifted the chip complex even as GPU names lagged.

long.h: Microsoft Suffers Historic June Rout as Investors Balk at Heavy AI Capex
long.p: Microsoft fell again as investors questioned a capital-intensity cycle projected to push capex toward 190 billion dollars, with Stifel cutting its target to 400 dollars. The hyperscaler de-rating spread to Amazon and other megacaps funding the AI buildout.

long.h: Why Is Nvidia So Cheap? Up Just 4% This Year as EPS Is Set to Nearly Double
long.p: Nvidia has badly lagged the S&P 500 and its semiconductor peers in 2026 despite Wall Street expecting earnings to nearly double. The valuation gap frames a long-term debate over whether GPU leaders or memory names capture the next leg of AI spend.

long.h: Intel's Chip Business Shows Signs of Life After Years of Struggle
long.p: Intel, the centerpiece of a US drive to make more chips domestically, is showing early signs of a turnaround though a full recovery remains distant. The stock has stabilized well off its lows as the foundry narrative slowly improves.

short.h: Apple Dives 6% Below a Key Level After Price-Hike Margin Scare
short.p: Apple closed down 6.12 percent at 275.15 dollars, its worst session in months, slipping below a key technical level as investors weighed thinner margins. Some buyers stepped in, arguing the memory-cost hit is temporary.

short.h: Nasdaq Slips Even as Micron Soars; Semis Rebound on Memory Strength
short.p: Micron surged to new highs on earnings and dragged the semis ETF up 2.9 percent, even as the broader Nasdaq slipped. The split shows memory leadership pulling away from GPU and software names.

short.h: Microsoft Falls 3.5% as Stifel Cuts Target to $400 on AI Spending
short.p: Microsoft closed down 3.45 percent at 352.83 dollars as analysts flagged cloud-margin pressure from AI investment. Stifel trimmed its price target to 400 dollars while keeping a Hold rating.

short.h: Amazon Drops 3.1%, Underperforming the Broader Market
short.p: Amazon closed at 227.01 dollars, down 3.1 percent, as the megacap de-rating extended to AWS-driven names. Bulls still point to cloud and custom AI chips as the long-term reason to own it.

short.h: US Stocks Mixed as Micron Surges and Apple Slumps; Dow Edges Up 0.14%
short.p: The Dow rose 0.14 percent while the Nasdaq slipped and the S&P was nearly flat, with fresh inflation data weighing on sentiment. The orderly tape and a VIX near 19.7 pointed to rotation rather than broad fear.

notes.NVDA: Nvidia at $195.74, down 1.6 percent and lagging the semis rebound as memory names led; still up only about 4 percent this year despite EPS set to nearly double.
notes.MSFT: Microsoft at $352.83, down 3.5 percent in a historic June rout as investors balk at AI capex; Stifel cut its target to $400 on cloud-margin pressure.
notes.INTC: Intel at $132.87, up 0.9 percent and stabilizing well off its lows as a NYTimes piece flags early signs of a foundry turnaround; still highly volatile.
notes.AVGO: Broadcom at $378.91, down 0.8 percent as custom-AI-chip demand stays strong but GPU and accelerator names lagged the memory-led tape.
notes.QQQ: Nasdaq-100 ETF at $716.38, up 0.8 percent even as megacaps sold off, helped by a sharp rebound in semiconductors.
notes.SMH: Semiconductor ETF at $636.88, up 2.9 percent and leading the tape after Micron's blowout quarter validated the AI memory super-cycle.
notes.SPY: S&P 500 ETF at $734.30, up 0.1 percent and essentially flat as defensive and industrial strength offset megacap-tech weakness.
notes.AAPL: Apple at $275.15, down 6.1 percent after raising device prices up to 20 percent to offset surging memory costs, slipping below a key technical level.
notes.AMZN: Amazon at $227.01, down 3.1 percent as the hyperscaler de-rating extended to AWS-driven names; long-term cloud and custom-chip story intact.
notes.META: Meta at $542.87, down 2.7 percent, caught in the megacap-tech selloff tied to AI-spend concerns.
notes.GOOGL: Alphabet at $343.71, down 0.5 percent and holding up better than peers as its AI and search franchise stayed relatively resilient.
notes.TSLA: Tesla at $375.12, roughly flat at down 0.1 percent, consolidating after a volatile stretch with no fresh company catalyst.
notes.NFLX: Netflix at $70.90, down 1.3 percent, drifting lower with the broad tech tape on no company-specific news.
notes.DIS: Disney at $98.05, down 3.0 percent and back below $100 amid broad consumer and media softness.
notes.BRKB: Berkshire Hathaway B at $488.21, down 1.3 percent but holding its role as defensive ballast through the tech selloff.
notes.DIA: Dow ETF at $519.26, up 0.1 percent as industrials and value names offset megacap-tech weakness.
notes.OIL: US Oil Fund at $109.31, up 2.8 percent in a rebound after a multi-day slide as the Mideast risk premium stabilized.
notes.BTC: Bitcoin near $59,704, down 2.1 percent and slipping back below $60k as risk-off sentiment hit crypto.
notes.GOLD: Gold near $4,048, up 1.0 percent and firming after its recent pullback as a modest haven bid returned.
notes.SILVER: Silver near $58.36, up 0.5 percent and stabilizing alongside gold after a steep multi-day decline.
